title: DeadEnd
description: A single dead end log entry — an approach an AI agent tried and abandoned.

repo.description: Repository identifier (e.g., 'django/django', 'pallets/flask'). The primary grouping key.

path.description: File or directory path prefix where the approach was attempted. Enables scoped queries.

approach.description: What the agent tried. Be specific: name the function, API, pattern, or file. This is the primary search field.
examples: monkeypatching Query._execute to inject custom SQL
subclassing ModelAdmin.get_queryset to filter by request.user
using raw() with string interpolation for dynamic table names

reason.description: Why the approach failed or was abandoned. Structured as a single sentence.
examples: breaks transaction isolation in nested atomic blocks
get_queryset is not called during bulk operations
raw() does not support parameterized table names, leads to SQL injection

turns_wasted.description: Number of LLM turns the agent spent on this approach before abandoning it. The economic cost signal.

agent.description: Agent harness that produced this dead end.

version.description: Repository version, tag, or commit SHA. Prevents stale dead ends from transferring incorrectly.

task_id.description: External task identifier for traceability (e.g., SWE-bench task ID, GitHub issue number).
